name: Build triage-agent skeleton
description: implement phase 2 from plan.md. phase 1 is already completed.

goal.answer: Implement Phase 2 of the observability incident-triage agent: create a new triage-agent Spring Boot service that acts as a Spring AI MCP client connecting to the Grafana and Jaeger MCP servers, wires an OpenAI model, implements grounding via Prometheus discovery tools, exposes a REST endpoint for triage questions, and passes a smoke test.

criteria.answer:
New triage-agent Gradle module exists, compiles with ./gradlew :triage-agent:build, and is wired into settings.gradle
MCP client auto-configures SSE connections to Grafana MCP (http://mcp-grafana:8000) and Jaeger MCP (http://jaeger-mcp-service:8080)
OpenAI model wired via spring-ai-starter-model-openai; API key from OPENAI_API_KEY env var
Grounding system prompt instructs the model to use Grafana MCP discovery tools (list_prometheus_metric_names, list_prometheus_label_names, list_prometheus_label_values, list_datasources) before generating PromQL/LogQL
POST /api/triage/investigate endpoint accepts a JSON question and returns the model response
JUnit context-load test verifies ChatClient and ToolCallbackProvider beans are created at startup
Shell script scripts/triage-agent-smoke-test.sh executes against the running agent (health + sample question)
triage-agent container added to docker-compose.yml on the observability network

constraints.answer: Spring Boot 3.4, Java 17, Spring AI 1.1.4, Read-only by design — agent must not expose write or remediation tools, Reuse existing Docker observability network and service names, Follow existing package conventions (com.example.*) and Gradle patterns, Do not modify Phase 0/1 services or infrastructure except to add the new module to compose

assumptions.answer: OpenAI API key supplied at runtime via OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable; Grafana MCP server and Jaeger MCP server are already running on the Docker network (Phase 1 complete); Both MCP servers expose SSE on the default /sse endpoint path; Model selection defaults to Spring AI OpenAI starter default and can be overridden via config

phases.name: Build triage-agent skeleton
phases.goal: Create, wire, and verify the triage-agent Spring Boot service as an MCP client with OpenAI model, grounding prompt, REST endpoint, and smoke tests.
phases.description: A single vertical slice covering module scaffolding, Spring AI MCP client configuration, OpenAI wiring, REST endpoint, Docker integration, and smoke tests.

steps.description: Scaffold triage-agent/ module: build.gradle with Spring Boot, spring-ai-starter-mcp-client, spring-ai-starter-model-openai, actuator, web; src/main/java/com/example/triageagent/ package; main class; application.yml with SSE connections to Grafana MCP and Jaeger MCP, OpenAI API key, toolcallback enabled, server port 8080.

verification.command: test -f triage-agent/build.gradle && grep -q 'spring-ai-starter-mcp-client' triage-agent/build.gradle && grep -q 'spring-ai-starter-model-openai' triage-agent/build.gradle

steps.description: Implement TriageService with ChatClient, a system prompt enforcing grounding discipline (discover metrics/labels first), and a user prompt template. Implement TriageController with POST /api/triage/investigate accepting TriageRequest and returning TriageResponse.

Add Dockerfile for triage-agent (eclipse-temurin:17-jre, port 8080) and add service to docker-compose.yml on observability network with OPENAI_API_KEY env var, depending on mcp-grafana and jaeger-mcp-service. Map port 8084:8080.
Add JUnit context-load test verifying ChatClient and ToolCallbackProvider beans exist at startup. Add unit tests for TriageController request/response mapping.
Add scripts/triage-agent-smoke-test.sh that checks agent health (/actuator/health) and sends a sample triage question to /api/triage/investigate, asserting HTTP 200 and non-empty response.

phases.summary: Phase 1 complete. The triage-agent Spring Boot module was scaffolded, wired into the Gradle build, configured as a Spring AI MCP client with OpenAI model, implemented with a grounding-enforcing TriageService and REST endpoint, Dockerized and added to docker-compose, covered by passing JUnit tests, and accompanied by a shell smoke-test script.

goal: Implement Phase 2 of the observability incident-triage agent: create a new triage-agent Spring Boot service that acts as a Spring AI MCP client connecting to the Grafana and Jaeger MCP servers, wires an OpenAI model, implements grounding via Prometheus discovery tools, exposes a REST endpoint for triage questions, and passes a smoke test.

Grounding system prompt instructs the model to use Grafana MCP discovery tools (list_prometheus_metric_names, list_prometheus_label_names, list_prometheus_label_values, list_datasources) before generating PromQL/LogQL
POST /api/triage/investigate endpoint accepts a JSON question and returns the model response
JUnit context-load test verifies ChatClient and ToolCallbackProvider beans are created at startup
Shell script scripts/triage-agent-smoke-test.sh executes against the running agent (health + sample question)
triage-agent container added to docker-compose.yml on the observability network
constraints: Read-only by design — agent must not expose write or remediation tools
Reuse existing Docker observability network and service names
Follow existing package conventions (com.example.*) and Gradle patterns
Do not modify Phase 0/1 services or infrastructure except to add the new module to compose
